title: Kitchen Island with Open Shelving
summary: A full multi-bay built-in island — a sink base, dishwasher panel, trash pull-out, and open shelving joined into one plywood carcass, finished with corbels under a fabricator-cut countertop.
description:
This is a genuinely large built-in project, not a standalone furniture piece — four separate bays (sink base, trash pull-out, dishwasher panel, and open shelving) are built as individual plywood cabinets, then joined into one continuous run behind a single back panel. Build the sink base first: cut 4"x4" toekicks into the front edges with a jigsaw, attach the shelf flush to the toekick cutout with pocket holes, then add three bay supports for strength — note that the top bay supports run vertically here specifically to clear a modern oversized sink.

**Double-check your actual dishwasher's clearance requirement before cutting the dishwasher panel** — 24" is standard, but not universal, and some dishwashers need more room than the plan assumes. The plan also flags that the top-front bay support on this panel may need to be skipped depending on that clearance, with a bottom-back support added instead for strength.

Once all four bays are built, the single back panel spans and ties them together — glued, brad-nailed, and reinforced with screws through every joint. Trim (top, bottom, and stile boards) wraps the whole assembled run, with 1x3 stiles at the outer corners specifically because the corbels need a wider board to land on than a 1x2 provides. **The countertop itself is sourced and fabricated separately** — this plan builds the cabinet base only, not the counter, and the corbels go on last, secured from the back side once the countertop is in place.
timeLabel: 15–24 hrs across multiple days, plus countertop fabrication lead time

materials.name: 1x3, 8 ft, furring strips for bay supports
Double trash pull-out, max 15 in width
Countertop, sourced and fabricated separately (not included in this build's cost)
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Wider stile so the corbels land on solid trim.
Measure and cut to fit after installing drawer sides.

steps.title: Break down the sheets and cut the trim
steps.body: Lay every 3/4" plywood part out on both sheets before the first cut — five cabinet side/shelf panels at 23 1/4" x 34 1/2", the 23 1/4" x 15" trash pull-out shelf, four open shelf panels at 11 1/4" x 34 1/2", six open shelf boards at 11 1/4" x 24 1/2", the 23 1/4" x 15 1/2" trash door and two sink-base doors at 17 3/8" x 29 1/2" together consume almost the entire area of the two sheets, so one careless rip can cost you a whole sheet and you should budget on buying door material separately if your layout comes up short. Rip to width on the table saw and crosscut the long pieces with the circular saw against a clamped guide. Cut the 1/2" back panel 76 1/2" x 34 1/2"; that 76 1/2" is the finished length of the whole run, and the top trim, the top trim cap and the baseboard all repeat it, so those four numbers check each other. From the solid stock cut two side top trim and two side top trim caps at 35 3/4", four front stiles and four mid stiles at 27 1/2", four outer corner stiles at 27 1/2" from the wider 1x3 so the corbels land on solid trim, and the dishwasher end panel trim at 34 1/2". Edge band the front exposed edges of the panels now, while they are still loose and flat.

steps.title: Build the sink base
steps.body: Cut 4"x4" toekicks into the front edges with a jigsaw. Attach the shelf to the sides with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, flush to the toekick cutout. Cut and attach three bay supports, running the top supports vertically for sink clearance.
materials: 1x3, 8 ft, furring strips for bay supports

steps.title: Build the trash pull-out cabinet
steps.body: Repeat the sink base steps for the trash cabinet panel and shelf, strengthening with bay supports.
materials: 1x3, 8 ft, furring strips for bay supports

steps.title: Build the dishwasher panel
steps.body: Confirm your dishwasher's actual clearance requirement (24" is standard, some need more). Build the panel and add bay supports, skipping the top-front support if your clearance requires it and adding a bottom-back support instead.
materials: 1x3, 8 ft, furring strips for bay supports

steps.title: Attach the back panel
steps.body: Cut the 1/2" plywood back with a circular saw and attach with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails, adding pocket hole screws at the joints for extra strength.

steps.title: Build the open shelf section
steps.body: Build the open shelf carcass with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, attaching through the bay supports to the back plywood. Add bay supports at the bottom as well.

steps.title: Add the front and side trim
steps.body: Measure, cut, and nail on the top and bottom trim, then the vertical stile trim on the front edges of the plywood. Repeat for the sides using the wider 1x3 stiles at the outer corners.

steps.title: Trim the dishwasher panel and base
steps.body: Cut and nail a 1x2 to trim the front edge of the dishwasher panel. Trim the entire base with 1x4 baseboard, measured and cut to fit.

steps.title: Build and install the drawer
steps.body: The drawer is 20" deep: the two 20" 1x4 sides run the FULL depth and the front and back fit BETWEEN them, so at the listed 12 1/2" the box comes out 14" wide overall — which is exactly the 14" x 20" plywood bottom, and that agreement is your check that you have it the right way round. Install the sides onto the full-extension slides inside the cavity first, then measure the actual gap between them and cut the front and back to that figure rather than to 12 1/2", since slide clearance varies by brand. Join the corners with 3/4" pocket holes and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. Cut the 1/2" plywood bottom to fit and attach it with glue and 1 1/4" brad nails, setting the box at a height the drawer face will cover.

steps.title: Attach faces and install doors
steps.body: Attach the drawer face to the drawer box. Install the doors with frameless half-overlay concealed hinges. Note: the listed "Drawer face, trash pull-out" is 3/4" x 6" x 15 1/2" from "1x6, 8 ft", but a 1x6 is only 5 1/2" actual width — rip a wider board or accept a 5 1/2" face; do not expect 6" from a 1x6.

steps.title: Install countertop and corbels
steps.body: Have the countertop fabricated and installed separately by a fabricator (this plan builds only the cabinet base). Once installed, attach the corbels from the back side with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Install the listed double trash pull-out hardware into the trash cabinet bay now (max 15 in width), following the manufacturer's mounting template — no step previously placed this hardware.
materials: Countertop, sourced and fabricated separately (not included in this build's cost)
Double trash pull-out, max 15 in width

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Built-in kitchen island with sink base, trash pull-out, dishwasher panel, and open shelving
